faculty.bio: With a firm aspiration to pursue a career in academia specializing in business and economics, He possesses a robust academic foundation spanning areas such as business negotiation, corporate innovation, marketing strategy, political economy, institutions, and corporate policies. He harbors a keen research interest in analyzing the intricacies of how states, national, and international organizations conduct business, along with the profound impact of political and corporate policies on consumer markets. Backed by professional and academic business negotiation certifications, along with hands-on experience in trade marketing, He is deeply committed to exploring efficient pedagogical methods for imparting knowledge to undergraduate and postgraduate students. His vision involves creating a symbiotic relationship between industry and academia, particularly in lecturing on the fundamental principles of marketing, strategic management, business communication, business environment, and international business. He aspires to cultivate a philosophical understanding of business and economics, driven by the ambition to become a lifelong contributor to these dynamic fields.

faculty.bio: Jace Saha is a research-oriented person who is currently serving as Assistant Professor of Finance and Banking at Metropolitan University, Sylhet .She also served as Senior Lecturer and Lecturer at the same department before. Ms. Saha is deeply committed to conducting high-quality researches and providing high quality education to the students. Her research interests include; economic growth and inequality, modern finance theories, digital finance, corporate governance and sustainability of banking system. She has profound concern on the topic of financial technology (better known as fintech) for her higher study. She also has an intense concern to build up a prolific career through hard work, sincerity and innovative activities. Ms. Saha has many research works which have been published in peer-reviewed national and international journals according to her interest. She also actively participated in national and international research workshops and training programs. Ms. Saha graduated from the Metropolitan University, Sylhet with a Master of Business Administration (MBA) degree in 2017 and a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) degree in 2016. Having completed her Graduation (BBA) and Post Graduation (MBA), major in Finance and Banking she secured 1st position in BBA. She was awarded the Chancellor’s Gold medal, summa cum laude award for outstanding performance and Chairman’s merit scholarship for her academic performance. During her university life, she participated in a variety of extracurricular activities. She has a rational mentality to take action any communal issues.

faculty.bio: Md. Alaul Haque is currently working as an Associate Professor (Accounting and Information Systems) at the Department of Business Administration in Metropolitan University. Earlier from 12th September, 2015 to 11th September 2017 he worked as a Lecturer, September 12, 2017 to February 28, 2020 as a Senior Lecturer and from 1st March, 2020 to 9th March, 2024 as an Assistant Professor (Accounting and Information Systems) at the Department of Business Administration in Metropolitan University. He has been in teaching profession over the last 9 years or so. Mr. Haque is always very active persons of Curricular, Co-Curricular and extra-Curricular activities of this University. Now at present, Mr. Haque performs his duty in Metropolitan University Accounting Association as a President over 5 years and earlier he was the Treasurer of first committee of MU AIS Association (MUAA). Mr. Haque is a running President of MU E-Business Club (MUEC) and earlier he was the Treasurer of Metropolitan University e-Business Club also. He also performs his duty as a Treasurer of Metropolitan University Social Services Club (MUSSC). Mr. Haque is a member of curriculum committee and Exam Coordinator (Supplementary) of Department of Business Administration. He was born in Rajbari District of Bangladesh in 18th December, 1989. He is only one child of his parent. His father name is Md. Abdur Rob and his mother name is Hajera Begum. He is always positive attitude to his responsibility and a friendly mentality with smiling face. He also has a good leading skill, convincing and influencing power. Recently, Mr. Haque pursue an MPhil degree from the Department of Business Administration in Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST). The title of Mr. Haque's thesis is " Effect of Corporate Governance on Capital Structure Decision: An Empirical Study on Textile Sector of Bangladesh". Earlier Mr. Haque achieved his Graduation, Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) and Post Graduation, Master of Business Administration (MBA) in Accounting and Information Systems from the Department of Accounting and Information Systems in the University of Rajshahi. Earlier he completed his Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) in Business Studies Group from Machpara College and Secondary School Certificate (SSC) in Science Group from Machpara High School. Mr. Alaul Haque is a Funder and Manager of the “Folimara Bangabandhu Somobai Samite.” He also a Funder Manager of “Folimara and Vagbishnopur Boys Somobay Samite.” Mr. Haque is a Life member of Rajshahi University Accounting Alumni Association (RUAAA) and he was also a Member of BFDF in University of Rajshahi. He was a member of voluntary blood donor organization named “Badhon” in University of Rajshahi. His area of interest regarding research is Accounting, Corporate Governance, Finance, Banking and Social Science.

faculty.bio: Md. Emran Uddin is a Marketing Graduate who likes to apply marketing in his profession. He engaged himself in various social organizations where he tries to contribute to the development of the society. Emran has experience in doing various innovative projects. His noteworthy creative and innovative projects include “Fish Scale Branding”, “E-tourism” and “Societal Marketing for Rickshaw Puller”. Emran is primarily a marketing professional but he also has interest in Entrepreneurship. He always search for new businesses and tries to find solution for the existing business and economic problem. For higher study, Emran has grown his interest in the field of Economics. Most of the research work done by him is a reflection of his interest. As an academician Emran has got exposure to various international conferences in India. He has actively participated and presented numerous research papers in some of the renowned Universities in India. Among them Utkal University, Delhi University, Burdwan University, University of Jammu are notable as they are famous in their respective region. Emran also got the opportunity to work with Professors in India and Pakistan. Emran has published his research works in National and International peer reviewed journals. Having completed his Graduation (BBA) and Post Graduation (MBA), major in Marketing from the University of Dhaka, Emran secured 10th position in BBA. Securing a band score of 7.5 ensures that Emran is pretty much proficient in English. 550 in GMAT and a score of 5 in analytical writing ensures Emran’s ability in mathematics and decision making.

faculty.bio: In the year of 1988 he was born in Brahmanbaria which is called the cultural capital of Bangladesh. His childhood was made tremendous with the touch of placid breeze and stunning scenery of the river Titas. Yazdani received BBA and MBA degrees from Metropolitan University, Sylhet, a prominent university in this modern era in 2013 and 2014 respectively. During his studies, he was awarded with different scholarships such as Merit Scholarship and Chairman Scholarship. He was very keen to participate various competitive events and adorned securing superior positions in sports, debate, quiz competitions are foremost. In 2015, he joined the Department of Business Administration as a Lecturer and promoted to Senior Lecturer in January 2017. Yazdani’s main fields of interest are Contemporary Marketing Issues, Branding, Promotion, Access to Entrepreneurship Development and Impact of Technology on Business. He is the author of two peer-reviewed journal articles; one is published internationally and another in Metropolitan University Journal. His current research especially explores the branding and promotion strategies. He is an active member of LDF (Leadership Development Forum) Committee of Metropolitan University. From the dawn of his life he is confident in every sphere and he believes that charms strike the sight, but merit wins the soul.

faculty.bio: This is Mohammad Kamrul Ahsan, the research fellow of the Department of the Business Administration at Shahjalal University of Science and Technology. He has completed his BBA and MBA in Finance and Banking from the University of Rajshahi. His area of interest regarding research includes the Field of the Pattern of Human Saving Behavior, the Financial Statement Analysis, the Activities of Green Financing and the Activities of Green Banking. He has contributed several articles to the peer reviewed national and international journals. He has also attended several national and international research conferences at home and abroad. His basic strength is his honesty, responsibility and friendly nature. It is his passion to teach others. He always gets the pleasure to say something positive, to teach and to deliver an ethical speech. His happiness lies in the smile of his beloved students. Currently he is working as an Associate Professor (Finance and Banking) in the Department of Business Administration, Metropolitan University, Sylhet.

faculty.bio: Ms Nishath Anjum is working as an Assistant Professor in Department of Business Administration. She completed her graduation (BBA) in Management and post graduation (MBA) in Human Resource Management from Metropolitan University, Sylhet with outstanding CGPA. Earlier, she passed her SSC and HSC from Jalalabad Cantonment Public School & College, Sylhet with brilliant result. She always has the desire to be a teacher. By following her passion, she joined Metropolitan University in 2016 as a Lecturer and being promoted to the post of Senior Lecturer in 2018. She has done a few research work. Her area of research interest includes job attitudes, work stress, compensation & benefits, employee performance etc.

faculty.bio: Dr. Mohammad Jamal Uddin is a teaching professional having an expertise on Business Management, HRM, Econometrics and Research Methodology has been currently working as a Professor of the Department of Business Administration, Metropolitan University, Sylhet. Previously, he worked as a Principal at Kaimunnessa College, Narayanganj and Centre for Technology Transfer (CTT), Gazipur. He has been in teaching profession over the last 14 years in different educational organizations as Lecturer, Assistant Professor, Principal, Program Co-coordinator, Head-In-Charge and Head of the Department. Besides these positions he is also the member of Academic Council, Finance Committee, Proctorial Committee, Research Cell Coordinator, and so on. He completed the Doctor of Philosophy Degree (PhD.) on “Nature of Work-Family Conflict of Employees in Bangladesh and Its Management” from Islamic University, Kushtia, Bangladesh. He did his Graduation (BBA) and Post graduation (MBA) from the same university. Mohammad Jamal Uddin was 1st class 1st and faculty first in both BBA and MBA Programme. He was awarded Prime Minister Gold Medal for the brilliant result in BBA and Chancellor Gold Medal for MBA result. Earlier he passed his HSC & SSC from Dhaka Board with outstanding results. His educational background in Business Management & HRM has given him a wide foundation to approach many relevant topics. His research areas are Work-Family Conflict, Business Management, Human Resources Management, Organizational Behavior, Small & Medium Enterprises, Higher Education and recent trends in banking Organization. He contributed many regular peer-reviewed research articles and conference papers locally and abroad on contemporary business issues. Mohammad Jamal Uddin has participated many national trainings and workshops on Research Methods, Research Methodology, Econometrics, Statistical Package for Social Science (SPSS) and Structural Equation Modelling through AMOS. He is also a reviewer of many national and international journals.

faculty.bio: Abu Jafar Md Jakaria is currently serving as a Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering department at Metropolitan University, where he passionately shares his rich experience from the tech industry to inspire and educate his students. His teaching focuses on guiding students through hands-on research and innovative learning methods, preparing them to tackle real-world challenges. In the tech industry, Jakaria founded two startups: ShohozDeal Ltd. and DesnDev Tech. At ShohozDeal, he managed a team of over 60 employees, gaining significant leadership and operational management experience. His role at DesnDev Tech as both founder and Lead Software Engineer allowed him to stay at the forefront of technological advancements, which he integrates into his teaching to provide practical insights into programming and development. Currently, Jakaria is actively involved in building projects with his students and conducting research, further bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical application in the field of computer science.

faculty.bio: Mr. Akhlak Uz Zaman Ashik is an Assistant Professor in Computer Science and Engineering Department at Metropolitan University, Sylhet, Bangladesh. He has completed BSc in Computer Science and Engineering from Metropolitan University and is currently studying MSc in Information Technology at Jahangirnagar University, Savar, Dhaka. He worked as a Software Developer and Embedded Programmer in SUST Drone Team and Metropolitan University Innovation Laboratory. Some of the projects he was involved in are Electronic Voting Machine, Unmanned Underwater Vehicle, Digital Braille Reader, and Raspberry Pi Cluster Computer. He led the teams that developed Chashir Hashi, Bornali Bangla Keyboard, TexVoiCE, BornoporichoyAR, and Quiz Race smartphone application. He solved more than three hundred programming problems on different programming problem-solving websites. He has done a few team-based research works on some interesting topics published in different reputed journals and publications. He wants to pursue his passions and continue his research works.

faculty.bio: Professor Dr. Md. Nazrul Haque Chowdhury was born in Beanibazar, Sylhet. He passed SSC examination from Zakiganj Government High School with brilliant result in 1972 and passed HSC examination from Sylhet MC College in 1974. He was then admitted to the Department of Mathematics, Chittagong University and passed B. Sc Hons in 1977 and stood First Class First. He passed M. Sc in Mathematics along with the successful completion of thesis from the same University in 1978 and also stood First Class First. He obtained highest marks in both B. Sc Hons and M. Sc not only in the Department of Mathematics but also in the whole University. For these meritorious results, he achieved Honorable President’s Reception at Bangabhaban and was also selected for the Honorable President’s Sea-cruise for four days in 1981 in which he participated. He started his professional career as a Lecturer in Mathematics in Bajitpur Degree College, Kishorganj. Thereafter, he also served at Adamjee Cantonment College, Dhaka Cantonment (Non-government College). He competed for BCS-1985 and qualified. Then he joined the Government service as BCS (Education) Cadre. He taught Mathematics in various Graduate and Post-graduate level Colleges for 34 years including 9 years 4 months in Educational Administration. During Government service, he acquired M Phil degree in 1997 and PhD degree in January-2004 from Jamal Nazrul Islam Research Centre for Mathematical & Physical Sciences (RCMPS), Chittagong University, attaining scholarship from International Centre for Theoretical Physics, Italy. He feels proud of being the student of one of the renowned scientists in the world, Professor Jamal Nazrul Islam (D. Sc) and also UGC Professor, Dr. Abul Mansur Chowdhury. He was selected directly to the post of Professor of Mathematics from the post of Assistant Professor by Public Service Commission (PSC) in 10% quota (he is the only person of Mathematics in Bangladesh) and was appointed to this post. He got Selection Grade Professor Post (getting seniority from 2006) in 2013 on the recommendation of Superior Selection Board (SSB) and by the approval of the Honorable Prime Minister. For last 9 years 4 months of his government service, he was the Principal of Sylhet Government Women’s College (Post graduate level) and retired in February 2015. He joined as the Professor and Dean of the School of Science and Technology of Metropolitan University in April 2015. During Liberation War in 1971, he was a student of class X. He helped the freedom fighters in many aspects. While he was a University student, he won First Prizes in literary competitions (viz occasional essay competition, set speech, extempore speech etc) and participated in National TV Debate Competition and achieved the recognition of the best speaker. He is a man of study and research. His field of research is “Chaos in Dissipative Dynamical Systems”. His research papers were published in different journals and many of his mathematical, ethical and contemporary relevant articles were published in daily national newspapers and in different scientific and institutional magazines. He presented eight research papers in different International Mathematical Conferences and Bangladesh Mathematical Society Conferences. He visited the United Kingdom, Saudi Arabia, India and Qatar. In these countries, he visited Cambridge University, Oxford University, Calcutta University, Presidency University, Madinah Islamic University and Qatar University.

faculty.bio: Ruhul Amin received the Masters of Science (M.Sc.) and Bachelors of Science (B.Sc.) degree in Statistics from the Pabna University of Science & Technology (PUST), Pabna, Bangladesh. He joined the Deep Statistical Learning & Research Laboratory, PUST, in 2021, under the supervision of Dr. Shamim Reza, Professor, and In-Charge of the Lab, Dept. of Statistics, PUST. Recognized for his exceptional contributions, he has received prestigious accolades, including the National Science & Technology (NST) Fellowship from the Ministry of Science and Technology, Bangladesh, for his research work. His commitment to pioneering research is evident through his successful projects, such as "An Online Diagnosis of Osteoporosis Patients Grading Using Artificial Intelligence Tools," which was accepted as an Innovative Project at Pabna University of Science and Technology (PUST). Ruhul's scholarly impact extends to his publications in world reputable journals like IEEE Access, ELSEVIER, MDPI, and etc. Additionally, he serves as a reviewer for Elsevier and PLOS ONE journals, contributing his expertise to ensure the quality and rigor of scientific publications in his field. A sought-after speaker, Ruhul has presented his research findings at esteemed conferences and seminars, including the International Conference on the 4th Industrial Revolution (IC4IR) and Beyond, 2021. He is working on Sarcopenia diseases, liver disease diagnosis, handwritten digit recognition optimization, diabetes, and osteoporosis patient classification. His research interests include big data analysis, statistical learning algorithms, deep learning, health informatics, and the application of these techniques to the processing of medical images and healthcare data.

faculty.bio: Umme Humayara Manni is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Economics at Metropolitan University. She earned her Bachelor's degree in Economics from Khulna University, Bangladesh in 2007, followed by a Master’s degree in Economics from the University Brunei Darussalam, Brunei in 2012. She recently graduated with a PhD in Planning and Development Economics from the Faculty of Business, Economics, and Accountancy at University Malaysia Sabah, Malaysia, in 2024. Her professional experience includes a tenure at the Department of Business and Finance at the International Graduate Studies College, Brunei, where she worked for one and a half years. Subsequently, she dedicated seven years to the Department of Business Administration at North East University Bangladesh, Bangladesh. She has published papers in reputable international journals including International Journal of Energy Sector Management, Journal of Energy and Development, Asia Pacific Journal of Innovation and Entrepreneurship, Asian Research Policy and Malaysian Journal of Sustainable Environment. She primarily focuses her research on economic development, international trade, and resource economics.

faculty.bio: Md. Amzad Hossain is a dynamic academic and researcher, currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Economics at Metropolitan University, Sylhet. His career progression—from Lecturer to Senior Lecturer, and now Assistant Professor—reflects his commitment to academic excellence and professional growth. Mr. Hossain holds a Bachelor of Social Science (BSS) and a Master of Social Science (MSS) in Economics from Comilla University, Cumilla, Bangladesh. His solid academic foundation has supported his successful journey in higher education and research. With an impressive academic portfolio, Mr. Hossain has published three articles and presented six conference papers. Additionally, he has significant research experience, having contributed to numerous national and international projects as a Research Assistant (RA). His research interests span critical areas such as education, health, social inequalities, rural development, and climate change. In addition to his academic endeavors, Mr. Hossain is an active leader in the scouting community. He serves as the Rover Scout Leader of the Metropolitan University Rover Scout Group and holds the position of Joint Secretary of Sylhet Zilla Rover. These roles have allowed him to mentor youth, promote community service, and cultivate strong organizational and leadership skills. Mr. Hossain also has over five years of leadership experience in social organizations, equipping him with expertise in project planning, budget management, cost-benefit analysis, financial oversight, and program evaluation. His hands-on approach to implementing and monitoring development initiatives has positively impacted marginalized communities. Motivated by a passion for societal advancement, Mr. Hossain seeks to further his career with leading international research organizations as well as academic institutions. His unique blend of academic expertise, leadership experience, and commitment to social impact equips him to excel as a lead researcher addressing complex global challenges.

faculty.bio: Muhtasim Fuad Ahmed is a Lecturer in the Department of Economics at Metropolitan University, Sylhet, where he teaches courses in business and economics with an emphasis on building practical, analytical, and problem-solving skills among students. His academic training in Finance and Banking, combined with industry experience as an Operations Manager in the education sector and earlier work in accounting, allows him to connect financial theory with real-world decision-making in business settings. His teaching and research interests center on sustainable finance, ESG integration, and how financial decision-making can support responsible and inclusive growth. He is actively developing his research profile in areas such as macro-financial linkages, exchange rates, and the role of ESG in corporate finance, with the goal of contributing to impactful scholarship that informs both academic debate and policy discussions. In the classroom, he strongly believes in interactive, student-centered learning where lectures, cases, and projects encourage students to think entrepreneurially, apply concepts to local and global contexts, and develop skills that translate into careers in finance, business, and beyond.

faculty.bio: M. Jahirul Islam received his PhD and MASc in Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE) from University of Windsor, ON, Canada in 2010 and MASc. in ECE from Ryerson University, Toronto, ON, Canada in 2003 respectively. He did his BSc. (Hons’) and MSc. in Electronics and Computer Science (ECS) from Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Sylhet, Bangladesh in 1995 and 1996 respectively. Since 1997, he has been with the Computer Science and Engineering Department of SUST (Professor since 2015). Prof. Islam has authored and co-authored 23 journals and 10 conference papers in the area of image processing, computer vision, pattern recognition and document image analysis. Currently, he is the Licensed Professional Engineer (PEng.- PEO) in the province of Ontario, Canada. He is also a member of IEEE and the International Association of Engineers (IAENG). Prof. Islam also worked in Umm Al Qura University (UQU), Makkah, KSA and University of South Alabama, Mobile, AL, USA as an Assistant Professor. He worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at Ryerson University, Toronto, ON, Canada in Robotics, Mechatronics and Automation Laboratory (RMAL) and St. Michael Hospital, Toronto, ON, Canada. He received many prestigious awards and scholarships that includes but not limited to Ontario Graduate Scholarships (OGS), Casino Windsor Breast Cancer Research Scholarship and three technical paper awards.

faculty.bio: Mr. Mahim Ahmed is a dedicated Lecturer in the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering at Metropolitan University, Sylhet. Since August 2023, he has been imparting his knowledge in various theory and laboratory courses, including Power Electronics, Control Systems, and Microprocessor and Computer Interfacing. He holds a B.Sc. in Electrical & Electronic Engineering from Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Bangladesh. He completed his Higher Secondary School Certificate (HSC) Examination at Jalalabad Cantonment Public School & College, Sylhet, Bangladesh, and his Secondary School Certificate (SSC) Examination at Gangkul Panchagram Adarsha High School, Sylhet, Bangladesh, both were completed with a GPA of 5.00/5.00. Mr. Mahim has actively participated in several research projects focusing on the integration of renewable energy sources and smart grid technologies. Notably, he worked on a project developing a smart energy storage scheme for pico-grid environments and his undergraduate thesis on intelligent energy management for electric vehicles. His contributions have been recognized at international conferences, including the presentation of his work at the IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG) in 2023. He has also gained experience as a Research Assistant at the SUST Research Center and as an Industrial Trainee at the Training Institute of Chemical Industries (TICI), Narsingdi, Dhaka, Bangladesh.

faculty.bio: Md Hasanur Rahman Sohag is a dedicated electrical and electronic engineer with a passion for electronics that began in his school years. He earned his B.Sc. in Electrical and Electronic Engineering from Metropolitan University, Sylhet. Currently, he serves as a Lecturer in the Department of EEE at Metropolitan University, Sylhet, since August 2023. His role allows him to share his knowledge and inspire students who share his passion for the subject. His career journey includes significant experience at Cybernetics Robo Limite, where he worked as a Hardware Engineer and Product Designer from September 2018 to July 2023. Mr. Sohag also worked as a part-time PCB and Mechanical Design Engineer at CRUX from May 2017 to December 2020. His responsibilities included research and development of innovative and educational products and kits, circuit and PCB design, mechanical 3D design and 3D printing, and served as an Electronics & Robotics Instructor. His skills span various technical areas including hardware, electronics, basic robotics and automation, microcontroller platforms, IoT technology, and mechanical and 3D design and 3D printing. Mr. Sohag is proficient in software tools such as Autodesk Fusion 360, Autocad, KiCad, EasyEDA, Proteus, MATLAB & Simulink, Arduino, Ultimaker Cura. He also possesses programming skills in C, C++, Arduino, MATLAB, Assembly, HTML, CSS, Bootstrap. His ongoing learning in CircuitPython demonstrates his commitment to staying current with emerging technologies.

faculty.bio: Md. Imam Mahdi is a Lecturer in the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering at Metropolitan University, Sylhet, Bangladesh, where he has been serving since 2025. He obtained his B.E. degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) from The University of Burdwan, India, in 2022. He subsequently completed his M.Tech degree with a specialization in “Next Generation Communication and Networks” from the National Institute of Technology, Durgapur (NIT Dgp), India, in 2024. He was awarded a fully funded scholarship by the Indian Council for Cultural Relations (ICCR) for both his undergraduate and postgraduate studies. His research interests include satellite communication, wireless communication, IoT, digital twin technologies, machine learning, deep learning, and artificial intelligence. He is actively engaged in academic research and serves as a peer reviewer for two international journals. He has published research articles in peer-reviewed journals and conferences. His teaching responsibilities include Microprocessor & Interfacing, Digital Logic Design, Technical Writing, Basic Electronics, and other core electrical and electronic engineering courses.

faculty.bio: Nawshad Ahmed Chowdhury, Assistant Professor and Head, Department of EEE has born in Sylhet. He received the BSc. in EEE from East West University (EWU), Dhaka, in 2012 and enrolled in the MSc. in EECE in Military Institute of Science and Technology, Mirpur, Dhaka. He has completed his SSC and HSC form the Aided High School and M.C. College, respectively. In 2012, He joined the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering of Metropolitan University, Sylhet as a Teaching Assistant. In the year 2013, he has become the Lecturer of the same department and became the Senior Lecturer in 2015. His current research interest is Nanosansor based on Nanoteansistor. At present, he is working on the development of bio-sensor compatible to detect bio molecules such as bacteria, fungus etcetera from water. In addition to his research in nanoelectronics, he also has his footsteps on different research areas like power, renewable energy system and communication as well. Beyond the academic duties he is also concomitant with different student welfare and development activities in the University. Simultaneously, he is the Member Secretary of Leadership Development Forum (LDF), Rovers Scout Leader of Metropolitan University of Rover Scout Group and Award Leader of the Duke of Edinburgh Award Bangladesh.

faculty.bio: Anik Biswas is an academic administrator. He is an Associate professor of English at Metropolitan University, Sylhet, Bangladesh who has been serving at this university since 2012. He served as the Chairman of the department from 16 October 2017 to 30 June 2023. Biswas has over 15 years of experience in teaching and previously taught at Sylhet Engineering College and Blue-Bird School and College. He has some significant journal articles and book chapters to his credit. He is the editor of the Newsletter of Metropolitan University. Besides, he serves as the Editorial Assistant of Metropolitan University Journal. His research interests include ‘literature and films’, ‘translation studies’, ‘cultural studies’, and ‘theories in literature’.

faculty.bio: Farhana Khanom Joly is an esteemed lecturer in the Department of English at Metropolitan University, having joined the department in August 2022. She is from the scenic district of Sylhet in northern Bangladesh. Farhana's journey began at Metropolitan University, where she pursued her undergraduate and postgraduate studies. Her commitment to her alma mater showcases her unwavering dedication to both her personal and professional growth. Her academic interests span across various areas of literature, with a particular focus on American Literature, Postcolonial Literature, South Asian Literature, 20th Century Literature (Poetry), Metaphysical Poetry, Victorian Poetry, and Romantic Poetry. Farhana's expertise in these domains makes her an invaluable resource for students seeking a deeper understanding of these literary genres and themes. In addition to her academic pursuits, Farhana actively engages in various activities within the department and the university. Her involvement reflects her commitment to fostering a vibrant academic community. Whether it's organizing seminars, workshops, or participating in literary discussions, she consistently strives to create an enriching and interactive learning environment for her students and colleagues. Farhana Khanom Joly's passion for literature, combined with her dedication to teaching and her active involvement in the academic community, make her an outstanding lecturer. Her students and colleagues admire her knowledge, enthusiasm, and approachability. Through her expertise and contributions, she continues to shape the minds of aspiring literature enthusiasts and make a lasting impact on the field of English literature at Metropolitan University, Sylhet.

faculty.bio: Dr. Suresh Ranjan Basak is Professor of English and Dean, School of Humanities and Social Sciences, Metropolitan University, Sylhet. Widely known as an author, researcher and translator, Professor Basak has teaching experience of more than thirty -eight years both at home and abroad. He has so far published fifty articles in various journals (English and Bengali) and twenty-five books from renowned national ( including Bangla Academy) and international publishers. His E. M. Forster, Mulk Raj Anand and Gabriel Garcia Marquez: A postcolonial Reading of Inherited dichotomies is considered a seminal critical text on the area. He has been anthologized in UPL Selected Essays of Bangladesh 2001, Jibanananda Das : A Centenary Volume , Eds. Abdul Mannan Syed and Abul Hasnat, Shamsur Rahman Sharakgrontho, Ed. Professor Anisuzzaman etc. He is the Editor of Metropolitan University Journal of English and reviewer of many national and international journals including ISLE Journal (Interdisciplinary Studies on Literature and Environment published by Oxford University). His areas of research include Postcolonial literatures ((English, American and Latin American), Postmodern Literature, Diaspora, Eco-critical Literature, Translation Studies and Rabindranath Tagore.

faculty.bio: Snigdha Das is an Assistant Professor of English in Metropolitan University. She received her graduation and post-graduation in English from Shahjalal University of Science and Technology, Sylhet. Eventually, she engaged herself in teaching first at a college and then at Metropolitan University, Sylhet. She would like to think on absurd literature and engage herself in further research on the world of absurdity human beings live in. Das develops her passion in reading postmodern facets of human lives reflected in different literary pieces. She is also fervent to know the crises of wars and human beings’ exertion to survive and live a meaningful life. In addition to teaching, Das engages herself in writing research articles on her chosen fields. She is the daughter of Shuvrendu Das and Purabi Das, coming from a humble origin. She currently resides in Sylhet with her husband and two daughters in an extended family.

He has been trained by many prominent professors and researchers through the participation in a series of workshops and seminars held in many different parts of Asia. Ashraful’s research interest mainly revolves around International Law with special focus on “Conflict of Laws” and “International Commercial Law”.

faculty.bio: Gazi Saiful Hasan is currently working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Law and Justice, Metropolitan University, Sylhet. He started his teaching career at this University in 2010. Before joining this University, he was a practicing lawyer in Dhaka Judges’ Court. He completed Master of Laws (LL.M.) from the University of Rajshahi, Rajshahi, Bangladesh, in 2009. His areas of interest are: Environmental Law, Local Government, Intellectual Property Laws, and Clinical Legal Education. Hasan shared his research findings at the conferences held at Shahjalal University of Science and Technology, Sylhet, Bangladesh (2017), Banaras Hindu University, Uttar Pradesh, India (2013), Metropolitan University, Sylhet, Bangladesh (2012). He participated in many national training programmes and workshops on human rights law, environmental law, refugee law, and humanitarian law. Saiful Hasan has authored several articles on different socio-legal issues which have been published in national and international journals. He has also authored four books. He often writes in national and regional newspapers on various legal issues, and participates as a guest speaker in discussion programs on law in Bangladesh Betar, Sylhet. He is also a news reader (Bangla) of Bangladesh Betar, Sylhet.

faculty.bio: Md. Sher-E-Alam is currently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Law and Justice, Metropolitan University, Sylhet. He was born in Lalmonirhat, Bangladesh, in 1987. He received his LLB and LLM degrees from Islamic University, Kushtia, Bangladesh, in 2008 and 2009 respectively. In 2013, he joined the Department of Law and Justice as a Lecturer and became Senior Lecturer in January 2015. Mr. Alam’s main fields of interest are contemporary Human Rights Issues, Laws of Inheritance, Access to Justice and Good Governance, International Migration and Refugee Law, Comparative Law, Law of Evidence, etc. He is the author of two peer-reviewed journal articles and his current research especially explores the Muslim Inheritance and Prerogative Mercy exercised by the Supreme Authority. Mr. Alam is an registered advocate of Bangladesh Bar Council and a member of Lalmonirhat District Bar Association, Bangladesh.

faculty.bio: I am Syeda Nazmur Siha Muna working as a Lecturer at the Department of Law and Justice at Metropolitan University, Sylhet. As a person, I always wanted to give effect to the accumulation of my aspirations, academic experiences, sources of encouragement and years of efforts in the field of educational and social development. My academic journey started with my Bachelor of Laws (LLB) from the International Programmes of University of London. I previously worked as a legal associate of Barrister Tasmia Prodhan and Associates situated at the Honourable Supreme Court of Bangladesh. Later on, I pursued my Master of laws in International Laws from Bangladesh University of Professionals (BUP).Continuing my studies, I embarked on social development activities. I was a trainee of Bangladesh Youth Leadership Center (BYLC). To explore the multitude of creativity, innovation and entrepreneurship, I was awarded a partial scholarship and given a chance to pursue a specialised course on this particular area from Watson Institute, Colorado, USA. I have experience of working in Bangladesh Betar as a reciter in my student life. For my passion for writing features and articles, I work as a contributing writer to the Daily Observer, Dhaka Tribune, Dhaka Courier and UNB. In order to attain efficiency on the ground of ‘Taxation Laws’ of Bangladesh and ‘Islamic Laws of Inheritance’, I have completed a ‘Professional Practitioner’s Course on Income Tax’ by the Success Law Academy and ‘Practice Course on Succession Laws’ by Professor. Dr. Ekramul Haque Sir respectively. I am very passionate about Public Speaking, Debate & Parliamentary Debate. I had several opportunities to adjudicate Parliamentary Debate Sessions. In 2017,I founded a venture named, ‘Posse-ability’ with the supervision of the American Center of the U.S Embassy, Dhaka to help people suffering from Specific Learning Disorder With a view to creating social awareness. Working on this ground has given me an opportunity to represent my project and my country at the Impact Hub, Florida and at the Watson Summit at Naropa University in Boulder, Colorado.

faculty.bio: Professor Dr. Md. Nazrul Haque Chowdhury was born in Beanibazar, Sylhet . He passed SSC examination from Zakiganj Government High School with brilliant result in 1972 and passed HSC examination from Sylhet MC College in 1974. He was then admitted to the Department of Mathematics, Chittagong University and passed B. Sc Hons in 1977 and stood First Class First. He passed M. Sc in Mathematics along with the successful completion of thesis from the same University in 1978 and also stood First Class First. He obtained highest marks in both B. Sc Hons and M. Sc not only in the Department of Mathematics but also in the whole University. For these meritorious results, he achieved Honorable President’s Reception at Bangabhaban and was also selected for the Honorable President’s Sea-cruise for four days in 1981 in which he participated. He started his professional career as a Lecturer in Mathematics in Bajitpur Degree College, Kishorganj. Thereafter, he also served at Adamjee Cantonment College, Dhaka Cantonment (Non-government College). He competed for BCS-1985 and qualified. Then he joined the Government service as BCS (Education) Cadre. He taught Mathematics in vsrious Graduate and Post-graduate level Colleges for 34 years including 9 years 4 months in Educational Administration. During Government service, he acquired M Phil degree in 1997 and PhD degree in January-2004 from Jamal Nazrul Islam Research Centre for Mathematical & Physical Sciences (RCMPS), Chittagong University, attaining scholarship from International Centre for Theoretical Physics, Italy. He feels proud of being the student of one of the renowned scientists in the world, Professor Jamal Nazrul Islam (D. Sc) and also UGC Professor, Dr. Abul Mansur Chowdhury. He was selected directly to the post of Professor of Mathematics from the post of Assistant Professor by Public Service Commission (PSC) in 10% quota (he is the only person of Mathematics in Bangladesh) and was appointed to this post. He got Selection Grade Professor Post (getting seniority from 2006) in 2013 on the recommendation of Superior Selection Board (SSB) and by the approval of the Honorable Prime Minister. For last 9 years 4 months of his government service, he was the Principal of Sylhet Government Women’s College (Post graduate level) and retired in February 2015. He joined as the Professor and Dean of the School of Science and Technology of Metropolitan University in April 2015. During Liberation War in 1971, he was a student of class X. He helped the freedom fighters in many aspects. While he was a University student, he won First Prizes in literary competitions (viz occasional essay competition, set speech, extempore speech etc) and participated in National TV Debate Competition and achieved the recognition of the best speaker. He is a man of study and research. His field of research is “Chaos in Dissipative Dynamical Systems”. His research papers were published in different journals and many of his mathematical, ethical and contemporary relevant articles were published in daily national newspapers and in different scientific and institutional magazines. He presented eight research papers in different International Mathematical Conferences and Bangladesh Mathematical Society Conferences. He visited the United Kingdom, Saudi Arabia, India and Qatar. In these countries, he visited Cambridge University, Oxford University, Calcutta University, Presidency University, Madinah Islamic University and Qatar University.
